title: Replace blocking sync requests in fill_from_vault with httpx async
description: fill_from_vault() uses blocking sync requests.post() inside an async method. Replace with httpx.AsyncClient so it never blocks the event loop.
acceptance_criteria: fill_from_vault uses httpx.AsyncClient with async with
No 'import requests' in fill_from_vault scope
Exception is caught and logged gracefully
notes: httpx.AsyncClient confirmed in fill_from_vault. requests.post removed. httpx>=0.27.0 in requirements.txt.

title: Add DELETE /api/sessions/{id}, GET /api/metrics, POST /api/sessions/{id}/stop
description: Three missing REST endpoints for complete session lifecycle management.
acceptance_criteria: DELETE /api/sessions/{id} removes session from _sessions dict and returns 200
POST /api/sessions/{id}/stop marks session as stopped, returns 200
GET /api/metrics returns session counts (total/running/completed/failed/stopped) and active engine
notes: All 3 endpoints verified with FastAPI TestClient: GET /api/metrics (200), DELETE /api/sessions/{id} (404 for missing, correct), POST /api/sessions/{id}/stop (404 for missing, correct).

title: crawlee-jsdom-based headless (differential evasion)
description: Add Crawlee with JSdom + Arachni dynamic renderer as primary engine. Provides zero-API-call stealth DOM parsing that catches sites blocking all Chromium+Firefox signatures.
acceptance_criteria: Import crawlee from backend/requirements.txt resolves without conflict
crawlee-jsdom can fetch and parse a Cloudflare-challenged URL without raising
crawlee-scrapy handles AJAX-rendered content
Can be selected as an engine via BROWSER_ENGINE=crawlee env var

title: Full browser automation tier 1 (camoufox + Xvfb)
description: Ensure camoufox virtual display (Tier 1) is stable and preferred. Add undetected-chromedriver as Tier 4 fallback for sites that fingerprint Playwright/CDP specifically.
acceptance_criteria: camoufox virtual still launches and navigates (regression test)
undetected-chromedriver can be imported and instantiated as fallback
STEALTH_JS is injected on all engine tiers
Tier auto-fallback: if Tier 1 fails, try Tier 2, then Tier 3, then Tier 4
notes: camoufox-virtual tier confirmed working. Tier 4 (ucd) not functional due to chromedriver version mismatch (Chrome 147 vs available chromedriver). 3-tier system (camoufox-virtual, camoufox, chromium) fully operational.

title: Proxy integration (Bright Data, ScraperAPI, generic)
description: Full Phase 1 proxy system wired into browser context. Residential proxy rotation, sticky sessions, geo-targeting. Working but needs verification end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: PROXY_PROVIDER=brightdata with valid credentials routes traffic through Bright Data residential proxies
PROXY_PROVIDER=scraperapi with API key uses ScraperAPI proxy
PROXY_PROVIDER=generic with URL list rotates through pool
Proxy is applied to browser context on all tiers (camoufox, playwright, crawlee)
notes: proxy_manager.py fully built with ProxyManager class. Bright Data, ScraperAPI, generic proxy pool supported. proxy_manager imported in browser_agent.py (line 16), instantiated at line 537, applied to all browser context tiers (line 665).

title: TLS/JA3/JA4 fingerprint randomization
description: tls_fingerprint.py generates randomized browser profiles. Needs wiring into Playwright CDP to actually apply TLS fingerprints on TCP connections.
acceptance_criteria: tls_fingerprint.py generate_ja3() and generate_ja4() return valid fingerprints
4 browser profiles (chrome_win, chrome_mac, firefox_win, safari_mac) each with different cipher orderings
Fingerprint can be set per-session in Playwright via CDP client
Verify with https://httpbin.org/headers — User-Agent and TLS fingerprint differ from raw Chrome

title: Advanced stealth JS (chrome runtime, font fingerprinting, media codecs)
description: Fill gaps in STEALTH_JS: AudioContext fingerprint randomization, font enumeration spoofing, VideoCodecs/WebCodecs spoofing, Permission API spoofing, Canvas 2D gradient noise.
acceptance_criteria: AudioContext spoofed to return consistent fingerprint across sessions
navigator.permissions returns 'granted' for camera/mic without real prompts
CanvasRenderingContext2D.getImageData() adds per-call noise
enumerateDevices() returns empty array (no hardware enumeration)
notes: Extended STEALTH_JS applied to all tiers. Added: AudioContext spoofing, enumerateDevices fake, CanvasRenderingContext2D.getImageData noise, Navigator permissions lying, Battery API stub, MediaCapabilities spoofing, Screen orientation, SpeechSynthesis voices, DPR randomization, Touch points, CDP traces cleanup. Verified: webdriver=False, permissions API present, DPR spoofed on camoufox-virtual.

title: AI model routing (MiniMax thinking, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama)
description: Multi-model router with MiniMax thinking tokens handled correctly, OpenAI structured output, Anthropic tool use.
acceptance_criteria: MINIMAX_API_KEY routes to MiniMax with thinking tokens stripped from action output
OPENAI_API_KEY routes to OpenAI GPT-4o with function calling
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY routes to Claude with tool use
OLLAMA_BASE_URL routes to local Ollama
Model provider selectable via API param and settings UI
notes: ai_router.py built with full MiniMax/OpenAI/Anthropic/Ollama routing. call_minimax strips X-MiniMax-Thinking header. Wired to browser_agent._call_ai via router_call_ai. Provider auto-detected from env vars.

title: Multi-tab agent (spawn/close/switch/parallel navigation)
description: Tab manager: agent can open new tabs, switch between them, close them. Shared context across all open tabs. Each tab has its own URL, title, and action history.
acceptance_criteria: Agent can execute 'open new tab' action and switch to it
Agent can switch between tabs by index
Agent can close a tab and continue on remaining tab
All tabs' URLs and titles are accessible to AI in each prompt
notes: Multi-tab built: switch_to_tab (line 1792), open_tab (line 1806), close_tab (line 1820). MCP tools: browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_open_tab, browser_close_tab all in mcp_server.py. Tab URLs/titles exposed to AI via page_context.

title: Domain memory (learns site structure, auto-injects context)
description: After 2+ successful tasks on same domain, store DOM fingerprints + action sequences. On revisit, inject 'site context' — what selectors and actions worked before. SQLite-backed.
acceptance_criteria: After task on a domain, store domain_heuristics record in SQLite
On revisit to same domain, inject prior context into AI prompt
success_rate tracked per domain
'Forget this site' button clears stored heuristics
notes: domain_memory.py built with SQLite storage. _build_ai_messages injects learned context via domain_memory.get_context(). action_history.track_selector() records successful selectors per domain.

title: Workflow recorder (record actions as JSON, replay)
description: Record button: every browser action becomes a step in a workflow. Workflow saved to SQLite + downloadable JSON. Replay feeds steps + current DOM to AI for smart step-matching.
acceptance_criteria: Record mode: every action logged to workflow JSON
Workflow can be saved with a name
Workflow can be replayed on same or similar page
Export/import as JSON file
notes: workflow_recorder.py built with WorkflowRecorder class. Start/stop/save/load/list/replay/export/import all implemented. 5 MCP tools: workflow_start_recording, workflow_stop_recording, workflow_save, workflow_list, workflow_replay. Wired to browser_agent.recorder. SQLite-backed storage.

title: CAPTCHA solver integration (2Captcha, Anti-Captcha, CapSolver)
description: When CAPTCHA detected (not just iframe), route to CAPTCHA solving service. Support hCaptcha, reCAPTCHA v2/v3, Cloudflare Turnstile. Return solved token to page.
acceptance_criteria: CAPTCHA detection logs the type (hcaptcha/recaptcha/turnstile/cloudflare)
2Captcha API key in env routes CAPTCHA to 2Captcha for solving
Solved token submitted to CAPTCHA iframe
Agent continues after successful solve

title: Visual DOM diffing (screenshot before/after, PIL diff)
description: Before and after screenshot per action. PIL computes pixel diff. If diff < threshold, page didn't change → retry or wait. Classifies diff type (content/modal/nav/ad).
acceptance_criteria: Screenshot saved before and after each action
PIL Image diff computed between before/after
If no visual change detected, agent waits and retries
Diff type classification in step output
notes: visual_diff.py built with PIL/numpy pixel diff. Diff types: content/modal/nav/ad/minimal/none. Wired into action execution loop: before_ss captured, diff computed after each action, stored in step output as visual_diff field. Auto-wait retry (1.5s + 2s) on no-change detection.

title: Frontend supervisor dashboard (real-time oversight + control)
description: Route /supervisor — full dashboard for real-time agent oversight. Live step feed, pause/resume, session save/load, undo, export log, runtime stats.
acceptance_criteria: /supervisor page loads and shows agent status
Pause button stops agent after current step
Resume button continues from paused state
Session save/load works from supervisor UI

title: Credential vault + blind fill (TOTP 2FA)
description: Phase 5 vault: master key in OS keychain, credentials encrypted at rest. Domain-scoped. TOTP support. fill_from_vault returns creds directly to page form, never to AI.
acceptance_criteria: add_credential stores encrypted cred in vault
fill_from_vault(domain) returns username + password + TOTP to page
AI never sees password — goes direct from vault to form
TOTP codes generated correctly for time-based secrets
notes: credential_vault.py built with CredentialVault class. Stores creds encrypted (keyring-backed master key). Domain-scoped lookup. TOTP via pyotp (now() generation). fill_from_vault returns username/password/totp_code without going through AI. pyotp>=2.9.0 in requirements.txt.

title: MCP server (16 tools, stdio + HTTP transport)
description: Phase 6 MCP: 16 browser tools exposed via MCP protocol. Stdio transport for Claude Desktop/Code. HTTP for Hermes. Live session integration.
acceptance_criteria: python mcp_server.py runs and advertises 16 tools
MCP client can connect via stdio
HTTP /mcp endpoint executes single tool calls
browser_navigate, browser_snapshot, browser_click, browser_type work end-to-end
notes: mcp_server.py has 18 tools: browser_navigate, browser_snapshot, browser_click, browser_type, browser_press, browser_screenshot, browser_extract_content, browser_scroll, browser_back, browser_wait_for, browser_list_tabs, browser_switch_tab, browser_open_tab, browser_close_tab, browser_session_save, browser_session_load, browser_get_page_info, browser_undo. MCP server factory functions built.

title: API endpoints (health, execute, WebSocket, session, vault, history, supervisor)
description: Full REST + WebSocket API: health, execute, WebSocket agent stream, persistent sessions, vault, action history, supervisor controls. Phase 1-6 complete.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/health returns 200
POST /api/execute runs a task and streams steps via HTTP or WS
GET/POST/DELETE /api/persistent-sessions/{name}/* work
GET/POST /api/vault/* works
GET/POST /api/history/* works
POST /api/supervisor/pause|resume|undo work
notes: main.py has all endpoints: health, execute, WebSocket /ws/agent, sessions CRUD, vault CRUD, history, supervisor pause/resume. 714 lines of FastAPI endpoints.

title: Docker deployment (multi-stage build, docker-compose)
description: Phase 5 done but broken — docker-compose maps to wrong port. Fix: update next.config.ts rewrites to port 8001 for Docker, test full docker-compose up.
acceptance_criteria: docker-compose up builds and starts without error
Frontend at localhost:3000 proxies to backend at localhost:8001
Health check passes inside container
Named volumes persist sessions across restarts

title: Frontend UI (hero, agent dashboard, settings, supervisor, task input)
description: Full Next.js frontend: hero section, agent dashboard with mode selector, settings page with model config, supervisor page, task input with presets.
acceptance_criteria: http://localhost:3002 loads without error
Execute button calls /api/execute and streams steps
Settings page saves model config to localStorage
Quick presets (pizza form, login, job board) execute correctly

title: Fix broken Docker port mapping
description: docker-compose.yml has TODO noting next.config.ts rewrites /api to wrong port (9001 instead of 8001). Fix the rewrite and verify docker-compose up works.
acceptance_criteria: next.config.ts rewrites /api/* → localhost:8001 (not 9001)
docker-compose up starts successfully
curl localhost:3000/api/health returns 200 from backend
Browser agent executes a task inside Docker container
notes: Port 9001->8001 fix already applied to next.config.ts in prior session. Docker not available in current environment (no docker binary) so cannot verify container runs.

title: Fix .env not loaded (load_dotenv at top of main.py)
description: API keys won't load from .env if load_dotenv() isn't called at the top of main.py. Verify it's present and working.
acceptance_criteria: grep 'load_dotenv' backend/main.py returns a line near top of file
With .env set, API key is used without hardcoding
Without API key, agent falls back to rule-based mode gracefully
notes: load_dotenv() confirmed at top of main.py (line 6). Verified: .env would be loaded before any API key access.

title: Fix select_option — use XPath text match not value match
description: Current select_option tries to match by option value. Many sites use option text content, not value attribute. Switch to XPath contains(text(), ...) for robustness.
acceptance_criteria: select_option can select an option by its visible text content
Select by value still works as fallback
Multiple select options are surfaced in page state for AI to see
notes: select_option (line 1736) now: tries value first, falls back to label=raw_value. Explicit 'label=text' syntax also supported. Select options fully surfaced in AI page context via 'SELECT DROPDOWNS:' section with all option values and selected state.
